items.definition_en: Footlights are lights at the front edge of a theater stage.
items.example_en: The footlights shone brightly during the opening scene.

items.definition_en: A cue is a signal for someone to do something on stage.
items.example_en: The actor waited for his cue before entering.

items.definition_en: A spotlight is a powerful light used to focus on one part of the stage.
items.example_en: She stepped into the spotlight to deliver her lines.

items.definition_en: A stage manager is the person who coordinates all parts of a stage production.
items.example_en: The stage manager called everyone for rehearsal.

items.definition_en: An understudy is a person who learns another's role to fill in if needed.
items.example_en: The understudy performed because the main actor was ill.

items.definition_en: A dress rehearsal is a final practice with all costumes and props.
items.example_en: They had their dress rehearsal before the big premiere.

items.definition_en: The orchestra pit is the area where musicians play below the stage.
items.example_en: The orchestra pit was filled with talented musicians.

items.definition_en: A playbill is a booklet with information about a theater performance.
items.example_en: I picked up a playbill as I entered the theater.

items.definition_en: The leading role is the main character in a play or show.
items.example_en: She played the leading role in the new drama.

items.definition_en: A matinee is a performance held during the day, usually in the afternoon.
items.example_en: We attended a matinee because evening shows were sold out.

items.definition_en: A scene change is when the stage setting is altered during a performance.
items.example_en: The crew prepared quickly for each scene change.

items.definition_en: The wings are the areas on the sides of a stage hidden from the audience.
items.example_en: She waited in the wings for her turn to act.

items.definition_en: A costume designer is the person who creates outfits for performers.
items.example_en: The costume designer showed sketches for the new play.

items.definition_en: A set designer is the person who plans and creates stage settings.
items.example_en: The set designer built a realistic town square for the show.

items.definition_en: The green room is where performers wait before or after going on stage.
items.example_en: The actors relaxed in the green room before the performance.

items.definition_en: A monologue is a long speech by one actor during a play.
items.example_en: Her monologue moved the entire audience.

items.definition_en: A chorus line is a group of dancers who perform together on stage.
items.example_en: The chorus line added energy to the musical.

items.definition_en: Opening night is the first official public performance of a show.
items.example_en: Everyone was nervous on opening night.

items.definition_en: A box seat is a private area with a good view in a theater.
items.example_en: They enjoyed the play from a comfortable box seat.

items.definition_en: A line is a sentence that an actor must say during a play.
items.example_en: He forgot his line halfway through the scene.
